source.history_note: GSPC.json covers 1927-12-30 to 2026-06-29 (24739 rows). dashboard_build_spec.md's data-map table says '~1923+' but no series in this repo goes earlier than 1927-12-30 -- documented gap, not fabricated data. Both fixed GSC anchors (1929, 1932) fall inside the real range.

grand_supercycle.note: Locked per project convention -- not subject to promotion.

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1962-01-02 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.date_discrepancy_note: this source's actual extreme in the search window falls on 1929-09-16, not the commonly-cited 1929-09-03 -- reported as found, not silently corrected to the textbook date

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1962-01-02 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.date_discrepancy_note: this source's actual extreme in the search window falls on 1932-06-01, not the commonly-cited 1932-07-08 -- reported as found, not silently corrected to the textbook date

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1962-01-02 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, per locked project convention (dashboard_build_spec.md §9)

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Supercycle-degree advance in progress since the 1932 trough, per the locked GSC framing. NOT subdivided into Supercycle I-IV internal waves here -- the only pivot detector available (pivots.py 'slow' scale, Intermediate/40wk-18mo degree per its own SCALES table) does not resolve century-scale structure distinctly from intermediate-scale structure, so asserting specific Supercycle I-IV turn dates from it would overstate precision. This gap is intentionally left for human review on the deep-history panel (dashboard_build_spec.md §9) rather than papered over.

alternates.label: Prechter / Elliott Wave International (as summarized by Wikipedia): a Grand Supercycle wave V completing in the 21st century, to be followed by a Grand-Supercycle-degree corrective decline
alternates.caveat: Source article frames this critically -- it categorizes Grand Supercycle theory under "Pseudoscience"/"Pseudohistory" and notes the theory's proposed Grand-Supercycle bear-market start date has been revised repeatedly (1987 -> 2000 -> 2006) after each prior date was invalidated by the market continuing to advance. Treated here strictly as a candidate hypothesis per spec §3, not ground truth.

alternates.label: Dr. Arnout ter Schure, Investing.com (2025-08-22): Primary-degree wave V bull run from the March 2020 COVID low; Primary IV ended October 2022; black W-3 target approx. 7120 by spring 2026
alternates.caveat: This is a Primary-degree count, nested far below Supercycle. Included as a directly-cited, lower-degree data point for future confluence checking against the Supercycle read -- not itself a Supercycle proposal.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 1932 low would invalidate the entire GSC (V)/Supercycle-in-progress hypothesis.

trace.hurst.underlying_trend: lib.hurst_fld.underlying_trend(bars, cycle) per cycle -- Hickson Concept #10 time_translation nested inside; descriptive only, see MCCLELLAN_NARRATIVE_PROTOCOL.md §1's 'confluence not verdict' framing (same rule applies here)
trace.local_wave_counts: lib.ew_count.find_all_impulses(GSPC.json, scale) for grand_supercycle/supercycle/cycle/primary (pct-ZigZag) -- rule-validated, NOT the human-reviewed Supercycle degree; state='none' per-scale if nothing passes the 3 hard rules
